task_completion.description: Navigate to the Security Policies section in Eramba
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eramba web interface should be visible with the 'Security Policies' (or 'Policies') module open and active.

task_completion.description: Open the form to create a new security policy
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, dialog, or page titled 'Add', 'Create', or 'New Security Policy' should be visible on the screen.
The text 'Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy' must be typed into the Name, Title, or Index field of the policy creation form.

The Description field must contain the text: 'This policy establishes guidelines for employees who use personal devices to access company data and systems, ensuring security controls are in place to protect organizational information.'

task_completion.description: Set the policy status to Draft
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Status dropdown or selection field must be explicitly set to 'Draft'.

task_completion.description: Save the policy and verify its creation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The policy form should be submitted, and the new policy 'Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy' with a 'Draft' status should be visible in the Security Policies list, or a success notification confirming its creation should be displayed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Eramba web GUI to create the policy
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the Eramba web interface. There should be no terminal windows, MySQL command line interfaces, or database management tools (like phpMyAdmin) used to insert the record directly into the database.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Eramba application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI should match the Eramba GRC platform (menus, styling, and layout). It should not be a generic text editor, a locally created mock HTML file, or a note-taking app.

integrity.description: Verify the agent entered data into the correct distinct fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must place the Name, Description, and Status into their respective designated form fields, rather than pasting the entire task prompt into a single text box.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected policy name is exactly 'Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy'. The expected status is 'Draft' (which corresponds to the integer 0 in the backend database, but must appear as 'Draft' in the Eramba user interface).
